rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF FREDDIE PATRICK WILLIAMS JR
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Procedural issues have not been cleared: 1. Amended Petition to specify "Third" report in its caption. CRC 7.102. 2. Notice of Hearing with a copy of petition must be given all persons who are entitled. The remote appearance instruction needs to be given to the persons. LRSF 14.1.C.6). 3. Petition to identify who are entitled to notice. 4. Even though petition includes some account information, this petition is treated as report of status of administration. 5. Need Schedule B of Receipt. 6. Need Schedule C of disbursements. 7. Proposed order must be submitted before hearing. LRSF 14.7.A. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF WALTER ROBERT FRANKLIN SCHUCHARDT
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Appearance required. Issues to include: 1) ~$70k is being distributed to American Research Bureau ("ARB"), an heir finder. Court to determine whether any inquiry should be made under Probate Code 11604. The assignments are for $887 - $8,878 each. 2) An accountant was paid $4,327.15 for the estate accounting preparation, which is generally the responsibility of the personal representative. Petitioner requests that his statutory fees not be reduced as the accounting was complex. 3) Petitioner reimbursed himself a total of $1,517.49 for long distance travel expenses without a prior court order as required under LR 14.57C. Remote appearances may be made. Please review Local Rule 14.1. The following information pertains to appearances by Zoom: WHEN ENTERING "YOUR NAME" ON ZOOM, BOTH THE FIRST AND LAST NAME, AS WELL THE CASE NUMBER, MUST BE INCLUDED. Meeting ID: 160 225 4765 Password: 514879 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252, and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF EDMOND LOW NG
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/18/2023 (Wednesday). The following procedural issues to be cleared two weeks prior. (1) Need corrected Inventory and Appraisal that (a) sets forth the assets subject to this estate only and that (2) identify character of each assets. I&As filed 12/16/21 and 11/7/2022; however, some of assets have been disposed under Spousal Property Order. (2) Paragraph #20 states the final I&A value as $189,003.76; however, no such I&A on file. Unable to verify statutory compensation calculation. (3) No waiver of account from Raymond Ng. (4) Need verification of ending balance of assets. LRSF 14.34.D. (5) Clarification of cost advance by the attorney on 11/23/2020. No record of the payments in the court system. (6) Clarification if Administrator has filed Creditor's Claims for the expenditure being asked for reimbursement. PC sec. 9000(a)(3). Proof of proof of the payments of the expenditures made by Administrator. (7) Need a separate provision of the omnibus clause which may need to breakdown between community property and separate property for any later discovered assets. (8) Proposed distribution in prayer #7 to describe specific percentage/fractional share of individual recipient. (9) Proposed distribution to specify dollar amount to be distributed to each recipient. CRC 7.651(b). LRSF 14.35.I. (10) Proposed Order must be submitted prior to hearing. LRSF 14.7.A. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.)

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF DONALD ALLAN KENNEDY
rulings.Calendar Matter: * ERROR *
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. 1. Petition alleges that the conservator defers the information to the court appointed attorney; however, pursuant to Prob. Code 2540, a. Need allegation that the conservator has discussed the proposed sale with the conservatee. b. Need allegation if the conservatee supports or is opposed to the proposed sale. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

The following issues must be addressed: 1) A supplemental inventory and appraisal must be filed for "assets added to estate", as required by section 2613. 2) A receipt signed by the court-appointed attorney for the conservatee must be filed to confirm payment of her fees approved by the Court's 08/11/2023 order. 3) A receipt signed by the petitioner as trustee of the conservatee's trust must be filed to show the assets listed on the schedule of distributions.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF POY SIM GIN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/19/23. The following issues to be cleared seven days before the hearing. 1. Proposed distribution (1) to describe the assets subject to distribution and (2) to identify the name of trustee(s) of the beneficiary Trust. Proposed order to be amended accordingly. 2. Clarification of the statutory compensation calculation at the level of 2%. Paragraph #25 states the total fee base as $494,716.88. Then, the total amount is $12,894.34, but petition seeks $12,442.84. 3. Allegation if the estate funds are deposited in compliance with the FDIC limit. 4. Paragraphs relating to the trust in finding (proposed order para #16) appears inappropriate as the trust is not before the court. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE LILLIE COLLINS LIVING TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For An Order To Determine The Existence Of The Trust; For An Order Confirming Property Owned By The Trust; And For Appointment Of Successor
rulings.Rulings: Appearance required. Issues to include: 1) Petitioner requests that Settlor's trust, which original is lost and which the copy that Petitioner possesses contains missing pages, be confirmed as existing. 2) Petitioner requests confirmation that all personal and real property owned by Settlor is a trust asset, which is overly broad. Per second supplement filed on 6/20/23, Petitioner alleges that she has not been permitted access to any accounts without a court order and therefore she is unable to provide proof of titling or date of establishment. She further alleges that other assets are already titled in the name of the trust. 3) Notice of Hearing does not specify the assets requested to be confirmed. Remote appearances may be made. Please review Local Rule 14.1. The following information pertains to appearances by Zoom: WHEN ENTERING "YOUR NAME" ON ZOOM, BOTH THE FIRST AND LAST NAME, AS WELL THE CASE NUMBER, MUST BE INCLUDED. Meeting ID: 160 225 4765 Password: 514879 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252, and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above.
